High_Requirements: Show Critical Path; The critical path indicates which tasks will cause project delays if they are delayed.
Low_Requirements: GUI needs to provide ability to display critical path and make the changes affecting critical path updated.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, design a GUI feature that displays the critical path and updates it automatically when changes affect it.
Emphasis & Caution: Ensure real-time updates after any relevant change.
Things to Avoid: Do not require manual refresh.

High_Requirements: Add/Remove Task links; Tasks can be linked with one another to indicate the sequence in which they have to be performed. For example, if there is a link from task 1 to task 2 then task 2 can only be started after task 1 is completed. The start date of task 2 must thus be after the end date of task 1.
Low_Requirements: GUI needs to provide ability to show utilization of a person.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, design a GUI feature that shows the utilization of a person.
Emphasis & Caution: Display utilization clearly and accurately.
Things to Avoid: Do not show outdated or aggregated data incorrectly.

High_Requirements: Change Task Begin/End Times manually with user changes; Change the start or end date either by editing its properties or by dragging-and-dropping the bar in the GUI.
Low_Requirements: The human resource class contains name, phone, email, and holidays. The class provides functions to set and get these properties. A class is used to represent the holidays. There can be multiple holidays.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, define a human resource class with name, phone, email, and multiple holidays, including setter and getter functions.
Emphasis & Caution: Holidays must be represented by a separate class and support multiple entries.
Things to Avoid: Do not merge holiday data into primitive fields.

High_Requirements: Add/Remove Resources to Tasks Dependencies;Dependencies between tasks and resources indicate the resources who are supposed to complete a task. Each task could be worked on by multiple persons and each person could work on multiple tasks. Dependencies may also be annotated to capture how many hours each person is supposed to work on a task on a daily basis.
Low_Requirements: The manager of task hierarchy provides a method to move a task node to a specific position.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement a method in the task hierarchy manager to move a task node to a specific position.
Emphasis & Caution: Preserve task hierarchy integrity after moving.
Things to Avoid: Do not break parent-child relationships.

Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement a human resource class that manages multiple resource assignment objects and returns their list.
Emphasis & Caution: Ensure all assignments are retrievable.
Things to Avoid: Do not return partial or copied lists.

High_Requirements: Add/Remove Holidays and Vacation Days;Holidays and vacation days are properties of persons (resources). changing this information also changes the availability of a person on certain days.
Low_Requirements: A GUI class of graphic area provides function to draw dependency. The function uses an object of task manager to add dependencies.
Instruction:

Emphasis & Caution: Dependency creation must be handled through the task manager.
Things to Avoid: Do not create dependencies directly without the manager.

High_Requirements: Prevent Circular Dependencies;Links among tasks may not be circular in that task 1 cannot depend on task 2 if task 2 already depends on task 1.

Emphasis & Caution: Subtrees must be built based on dependencies.
Things to Avoid: Do not display a static or flat tree.
